serving.natural_language_portion: A typical raw serving of whole chicken with skin is approximately 150g, equivalent to a single bone-in thigh or a medium breast portion before cooking.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken, whole, raw, with skin — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database for 'Chicken, whole, raw, with skin' (cr_id: F11010035). Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, sugar, and select micronutrients supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for raw whole chicken with skin (FDC ID 171477) where HPB does not report those fields.

gi_source.title: N/A — Glycemic index not applicable for raw whole chicken with skin
gi_source.source_match_notes: Raw whole chicken with skin contains negligible carbohydrates (0.13g/100g). Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates and produces no meaningful glycaemic response. No GI value is assigned per Project Veritas protocol for low/zero-carbohydrate foods. University of Sydney GI Database consulted and confirms no GI value exists for raw whole chicken.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (70mg/100g) with favourable potassium (189mg/100g) ratio
hypertension.guidance: Raw whole chicken with skin is low in sodium at 70mg/100g and provides 189mg/100g potassium, yielding a favourable Na:K ratio; individuals with hypertension should remove the skin before or after cooking to reduce saturated fat intake and avoid high-sodium marinades, sauces, or brines during preparation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate (0.13g/100g) with no sugar; high protein supports satiety
type2_diabetes.guidance: With only 0.13g carbohydrate per 100g and zero sugar, raw whole chicken with skin has no meaningful glycaemic impact; individuals with type 2 diabetes should remove the skin before cooking to reduce total and saturated fat, which can worsen insulin resistance over time, and avoid sugar-based glazes or marinades.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate total fat (14.83g/100g) with saturated fat (3.79g/100g) primarily from the skin
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The skin contributes the majority of saturated fat in this food; individuals with cardiovascular disease should remove the skin before consumption to reduce saturated fat intake, choose lower-fat cooking methods such as grilling, baking, or steaming, and avoid deep-frying which generates pro-inflammatory advanced glycation end-products (AGEs).

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Absence of anti-inflammatory nutrients (omega-3, polyphenols, antioxidants, fibre) despite moderate saturated fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Although saturated fat at approximately 3.79g/100g is below the 5g/100g RA threshold, raw whole chicken with skin provides no positive anti-inflammatory factors; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should remove the skin, pair with anti-inflammatory ingredients such as turmeric, ginger, or garlic, and avoid deep-frying which generates AGEs that worsen joint inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken, whole, raw, with skin is an uncooked whole chicken carcass retaining its skin, encompassing all primary cuts including breast, thigh, drumstick, and wing portions. The raw flesh displays a pale pink colour with firm, moist texture, while the skin is pale yellow to white and slightly fatty. As a raw ingredient, it has not undergone any cooking, marinating, or processing. Per 100g, it provides 213 kcal of energy, 18.33g of protein, 14.83g of total fat (of which approximately 3.79g is saturated fat, concentrated primarily in the skin), and only 0.13g of carbohydrate with negligible sugar, making it essentially carbohydrate-free. Sodium is low at 70mg/100g and potassium is 189mg/100g. The food is a rich source of B vitamins including niacin (6.8mg), B6 (0.4mg), and B12 (0.3mcg), as well as zinc (1.44mg), selenium (17.8mcg), and phosphorus (147mg). Clinically, it is suitable for type 2 diabetes and hypertension management when the skin is removed before cooking, as the skin is the primary source of saturated fat. For cardiovascular disease, skin removal and low-fat cooking methods are strongly recommended. The food is naturally gluten-free, dairy-free, and suitable for halal and keto dietary patterns. It is widely used across global cuisines as a foundational protein ingredient for roasting, grilling, braising, poaching, and stewing preparations.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F11010035, confidence 0.95). Secondary micronutrient values supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171477) for raw whole chicken with skin, an approved primary source. Both sources are on the approved source list and the food identity is an exact match.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is 0.13g/100g — this is a naturally occurring trace amount in raw chicken, not zero; no zero-macro justification is required as the value is non-zero. Sugar_g is reported as 0.0g/100g: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171477) for raw whole chicken with skin. Dietary_fibre_g is 0.0g/100g: naturally zero as chicken is an animal-derived food containing no plant fibre. Glycemic index is set to -1 (numeric sentinel) as GI is not applicable for this food containing negligible carbohydrates (0.13g/100g); gi_category is 'not_applicable' per Project Veritas protocol for low/zero-carbohydrate foods. Vitamin_c_mg set to -1: not reported in either HPB or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for raw whole chicken with skin. Saturated fat (3.79g), monounsaturated fat (5.99g), polyunsaturated fat (3.21g), trans fat (0.06g), vitamin A (21mcg RAE), thiamine (0.06mg), riboflavin (0.14mg), niacin (6.8mg), B6 (0.4mg), B12 (0.3mcg), folate (5mcg DFE), vitamin D (0.1mcg), vitamin E (0.27mg), phosphorus (147mg), magnesium (20mg), zinc (1.44mg), selenium (17.8mcg), and water (65.99g) sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 171477 (Chicken, broilers or fryers, whole, raw) as HPB does not report these fields. This food is a generic raw ingredient record; no specific cuisine or regional origin applies. Ontology codes are defensible best-match entries; FoodOn URI and FoodEx2 code reflect raw whole chicken with skin classification.
